\subheader{SUMMARY}{Analytics Engineer with 10 years of experience building the data infrastructure that powers reliable reporting, experimentation, and machine learning at scale. Deep hands-on expertise in dbt, Snowflake, Airflow, and Prefect — with a strong track record of migrating legacy pipelines onto modern stacks, reducing costs, improving data quality, and enabling analyst self-service. Equally comfortable in Python, R, and SQL, with a product mindset that keeps engineering work grounded in business outcomes.
}

\section{Kano Apps - Casual Games \hfill
  \includegraphics[height=1em]{icons/logos/kano_logo.png}}
{\vspace{-0.2cm}}


\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2025-05 -- \\ 2026-02}
  {Senior Game Data Engineer \& BI Analyst}
  {Victoria, BC Canada}
  {Hired to modernize the studio’s analytics stack by migrating reporting and  transformation workflows from an undocumented, monolithic, spaghetti code  R-based platform to a more scalable Python-SQL based platform. \textbf{Responsibilities}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Added DBT to the platform to better model, scale, and optimize transformations across the analytics stack.


    \item Reworked, enhanced, and migrated numerous RShiny reports to AWS Quicksight, improving reliability, accessibility, and maintainability.


    \item Migrated parquet table ETLs into partitioned Apache Iceberg tables, added DBT-Athena to the new platform to better model, scale, and optimize transformations done originally in R scripts too large, old and unstructured for Gemini to handle.

    \begin{itemize}

      \item DBT models reduced numerous redundancies, and improved turnaround time, and .

      \item Preserved seamless service delivery and data continuity across migration.

      \item Using Partitioned Iceberg tables allowed for more incremental updates, improving monthly EC2 and Athena savings (23\%) and report turnaround time (13x).

    \end{itemize}


  \end{itemize}
\textbf{Outcomes}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Reduced daily report refresh times from up to 14 hours to less than 1 hour while lowering EC2 and Athena costs by migrating expensive R ETLs into optimized DBT-Athena SQL models on partitioned Parquet and Iceberg tables.

  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

\section{Kixeye Inc - Mobile Games \hfill
  \includegraphics[height=1em]{icons/logos/kixeye_horse_logo.png}}
{\vspace{-0.2cm}}


\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2022-02 -- \\ 2025-01}
  {Lead Data Analyst, Core Analytics Team Lead}
  {Victoria, BC Canada}
  {Led analytics engineering improvements for Kixeye's Core Analytics Team while managing analysts — consolidating BI tooling, cutting Snowflake costs, and standardizing data delivery pipelines. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Reduced monthly Snowflake warehousing costs by 45\% through ETL and orchestration optimization, saving an estimated \$130K annually.


    \item Independently migrated both Kixeye and Babil Games studios' Looker dashboards onto Stillfront's shared instance, reducing studio costs by \$90K annually while improving reporting continuity.


    \item Designed and iterated on analytics event schemas within a cross-studio SDK — identifying and correcting structural flaws in initial event definitions to ensure reliable cross-game player behavior tracking for promotion measurement.


    \item Assisted migrateing game analytics pipelines from Google Cloud Platform  to AWS — rebuilding streaming and batch data flows using Firehose, Lambda, and Kinesis to support the data infrastructure needs of two newly launched titles.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

{\vspace{-0.5cm}}

\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2021-01 -- \\ 2022-02}
  {Senior Product Analyst, Acting Core Analytics Team Lead}
  {Victoria, BC Canada}
  {Acting Core Analytics Team Lead, owning experimentation and KPI reporting ETL automation while building analytical infrastructure for product and design teams across multiple live game titles. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Automated KPI forecasting and A/B test reporting ETLs for Product and Design teams, improving experimentation support and freeing substantial analyst time.


    \item Designed the analytics event schema and data models powering the Alliance Recommendation Engine — translating raw player behavior events into engagement and spend features, contributing to a 7\% annual revenue improvement.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

{\vspace{-0.5cm}}

\begin{entrylist}
\entry
  {2019-08 -- \\ 2021-01}
  {Product Analyst, War Commander: Rogue Assault Team}
  {Vancouver, BC Canada}
  {Embedded analyst on War Commander — repairing legacy ETLs, migrating ML pipelines off Domino, and building reliable data infrastructure for the live game's product and UA teams. \textbf{Responsibilities \& Achievements}:
  \begin{itemize}

    \item Repaired and optimized numerous legacy ETL pipelines and dashboards, producing meaningful warehousing savings and improving trust in core reporting during first three months.


    \item Built and maintained LTV and revenue forecast models as stakeholder-facing data products — improving forecast accuracy and providing product and UA teams with reliable planning inputs.


    \item Migrated analytics ETLs, ML pipelines, and data science applications off Domino and onto Jenkins, reducing monthly platform costs by roughly \$27K.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}

  \begin{itemize}

    \item Built HiveQL and SQL analysis pipelines that powered A/B test evaluation for FIFA in-game messaging — providing repeatable, analyst-ready experiment readouts across campaigns.

  \end{itemize}}

\entry
  {2016-06 -- \\ 2018-11}
  {Business Analyst, FIFA Player Analytics Team}
  {Burnaby, BC Canada}

  \begin{itemize}

    \item Owned analytics reporting for FIFA's strategic licensing partners and automated large portions of the process using R and Talend, reducing weeks of manual work assembling 550 slides to seconds.


    \item Built statistical models to assess the value each license created for EA and the reciprocal value exposure provided to partners, helping improve licensing decisions.


  \end{itemize}}
\end{entrylist}
